title: NOC 32200 – Traditional Chinese Medicine Practitioners and Acupuncturists | GTR Immigration
description: Traditional Chinese Medicine Practitioners and Acupuncturists under NOC 32200 are regulated natural health professionals providing acupuncture, herbal medicine, and holistic therapeutic treatments across Canada. As demand for complementary and integrative healthcare continues to grow, this TEER 2 occupation offers expanding opportunities in both private and collaborative clinical settings. For qualified international practitioners, NOC 32200 may support eligibility under Express Entry and selected Provincial Nominee Programs, providing a pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group322 – Practitioners of natural healingMinor Group3220 – Practitioners of natural healingNOC Code32200

duties: Diagnose diseases and physiological disorders using Traditional Chinese Medicine frameworks
Explain procedures, risks, and benefits to patients
Administer treatments such as acupuncture, electro-acupuncture, TENS, cupping, moxibustion, Gua Sha, auriculotherapy
Prescribe herbal medicines and dietary supplements
Provide Tui Na, Qi Gong, Tai Ji Quan guidance
Collaborate with healthcare professionals when developing treatment plans
Diagnose imbalances according to Chinese medicine traditions
Develop treatment plans including acupuncture, acupressure, moxibustion, cupping, and electrical stimulation
employment_requirements: Traditional Chinese Medicine Practitioners
Completion of a four- to five-year diploma program in Traditional Chinese MedicineOR
Completion of recognized international medical training in TCM

salary_prospects: Annual income typically ranges from $42,400 to $105,900, particularly for experienced practitioners operating private clinics.
job_outlook: Growth in complementary and integrative medicine
Increased demand for non-invasive treatment options
Self-employed practitioners in metropolitan areas may experience stronger earning potential.
